# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 3

This is turn 3. Turns 1 through 2 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 3.

## Turn 3 — the principal-unit lift comes from the three-puncture cyclotomic action

Turn 2 reduced the repaired normalization to an automorphism powering the two associated procyclic subgroups of the HNN base by \(5\).  There is a uniform construction for every \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\).

Write
\[
D_0=\langle A,S,Y\mid A^2S^4[S,Y]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}.
\]
The relation is equivalently
\[
Y^{-1}SY=T,\qquad T=S^{-3}A^{-2}.
\]
Put \(P=S^3\). Since \(3\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), \(P,A\) are again a free pro-\(2\) basis of the HNN base. Now
\[
T=P^{-1}A^{-2},\qquad C:=(PT)^{-1}=A^2.
\]
Thus the awkward two-boundary pair is obtained by adjoining a square root of the third peripheral element in a three-puncture group:
\[
\Delta=\langle P,T\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2},\qquad C=(PT)^{-1},
\]
\[
E^\square
 =\langle P,T,A\mid PTA^2=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}
 \cong\langle P,A\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}.
\]

### Cyclotomic peripheral-power lemma

For every \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), there is an automorphism
\(\varphi_u\in\operatorname{Aut}(\Delta)\) such that
\[
\varphi_u(P)\sim P^u,\qquad
\varphi_u(T)\sim T^u,\qquad
\varphi_u(C)\sim C^u,
\]
where \(\sim\) denotes conjugacy.

Indeed, identify \(\Delta\) with the maximal pro-\(2\) geometric fundamental group of
\[
\mathbf P^1_{\overline{\mathbf Q}}\setminus\{0,1,\infty\},
\]
with \(P,T,C\) the three peripheral inertia generators.

Choose an automorphism of
\(\mathbf Q(\mu_{2^\infty})\) given by
\[
\zeta\longmapsto\zeta^u
\]
and extend it to \(\overline{\mathbf Q}\). Its outer action on the geometric fundamental group preserves each rational cusp. If \(z\) is a local parameter and a peripheral generator acts by
\[
z^{1/2^n}\longmapsto \zeta_{2^n}z^{1/2^n},
\]
then conjugating by this Galois element replaces \(\zeta_{2^n}\) by
\(\zeta_{2^n}^u\). Hence the peripheral generator is sent to its \(u\)-th power, up to the conjugacy arising from the choice of paths to the basepoint.

Choose a representative and write
\[
\varphi_u(P)=c_P^{-1}P^uc_P,\qquad
\varphi_u(T)=c_T^{-1}T^uc_T,\qquad
\varphi_u(C)=c_C^{-1}C^uc_C.
\]

### Lifting through the square root

Define an endomorphism of \(E^\square\) by
\[
P\longmapsto\varphi_u(P),\qquad
T\longmapsto\varphi_u(T),\qquad
A\longmapsto c_C^{-1}A^uc_C.
\]
It respects the relation because
\[
(c_C^{-1}A^uc_C)^2
  =c_C^{-1}A^{2u}c_C
  =c_C^{-1}C^uc_C
  =\varphi_u(C)
  =\bigl(\varphi_u(P)\varphi_u(T)\bigr)^{-1}.
\]

Modulo the Frattini subgroup, its images of the free basis \(P,A\) are
\[
\bar P\longmapsto u\bar P=\bar P,\qquad
\bar A\longmapsto u\bar A=\bar A,
\]
since \(u\) is odd. The pro-\(2\) Burnside basis theorem therefore makes this endomorphism surjective, hence an automorphism. Denote it by \(\theta_u\).

Because \(P=S^3\) and the cube map is an automorphism of every procyclic pro-\(2\) group,
\[
\theta_u(S)=c_P^{-1}S^uc_P,
\qquad
\theta_u(T)=c_T^{-1}T^uc_T.
\]

It consequently extends across the HNN relation by
\[
\Psi_u|_{\langle S,A\rangle}=\theta_u,\qquad
\Psi_u(Y)=c_P^{-1}Yc_T.
\]
Indeed,
\[
\Psi_u(Y)^{-1}\Psi_u(S)\Psi_u(Y)
 =c_T^{-1}Y^{-1}S^uYc_T
 =c_T^{-1}T^uc_T
 =\Psi_u(T).
\]
Again the induced Frattini map is invertible, so \(\Psi_u\) is an automorphism of \(D_0\).

This proves the entire relative-power lemma uniformly, including the \(u=5\) case isolated in Turn 2.

### The induced marked automorphisms

Put
\[
t=\bar A+2\bar S,\qquad
B=D_0^{\mathrm{ab}}
  \cong C_2t\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar S\oplus\mathbf Z_2\bar Y.
\]
The canonical orientation is
\[
\chi_0(t)=-1,\qquad
\chi_0(\bar S)=1,\qquad
\chi_0(\bar Y)=\eta:=(-3)^{-1}.
\]
Since \(\eta\) generates \(1+4\mathbf Z_2\),
\[
\ker\chi_0=\mathbf Z_2\bar S.
\]
Consequently
\[
\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)
 =
 \left\{
 \alpha_{u,b}:
 \begin{array}{l}
 t\mapsto t,\\
 \bar S\mapsto u\bar S,\\
 \bar Y\mapsto\bar Y+b\bar S
 \end{array}
 \ \middle|\ 
 u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times,\ b\in\mathbf Z_2
 \right\}.
\]

The automorphism \(\Psi_u\) constructed above induces some
\(\alpha_{u,b(u)}\): it sends \(\bar A,\bar S\) to \(u\bar A,u\bar S\), and its change in \(\bar Y\) lies in \(\ker\chi_0=\mathbf Z_2\bar S\).

The shear
\[
\Theta_b(A)=A^{S^b},\qquad
\Theta_b(S)=S,\qquad
\Theta_b(Y)=YS^b
\]
satisfies
\[
\Theta_b(A^2S^4[S,Y])
 =(A^2S^4[S,Y])^{S^b}
\]
and induces \(\alpha_{1,b}\). Therefore
\[
\Theta_{-b(u)}\Psi_u
\]
induces \(\alpha_{u,0}\), and all \(\alpha_{u,b}\) lift.

Hence
\[
\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\longrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)
\]
is surjective.

## Corrected Proposition 3.4

The original Proposition 3.4 is false because it omits the marked canonical orientation, as established in Turn 1.  Replace it by:

> Let \(D_r=F/\overline{\langle\!\langle r\rangle\!\rangle}\), with the other hypotheses of Proposition 3.4 unchanged. Assume additionally that the canonical Demushkin orientation, transported to the marked abelianization \(B\), is
> \[
> \chi_r(\bar A)=-1,\qquad
> \chi_r(\bar S)=1,\qquad
> \chi_r(\bar Y)=(-3)^{-1}.
> \]
> Then there is \(\varphi\in\operatorname{Aut}(F)\) with
> \[
> \rho\varphi=\rho,\qquad
> \overline{\langle\!\langle\varphi(r)\rangle\!\rangle}
> =
> \overline{\langle\!\langle r_0\rangle\!\rangle}.
> \]

Proof: Labute classification supplies an abstract isomorphism
\[
f:D_r\xrightarrow{\sim}D_0.
\]
Canonical orientations are functorial, so the induced automorphism
\[
f_{\mathrm{ab}}:B\longrightarrow B
\]
lies in \(\operatorname{Aut}(B,\chi_0)\). By the surjectivity just proved, compose \(f\) with an automorphism of \(D_0\) inducing \(f_{\mathrm{ab}}^{-1}\). The resulting isomorphism
\[
\beta:D_r\xrightarrow{\sim}D_0
\]
acts identically on \(B\).

Lift the three elements
\(\beta(q_r(A)),\beta(q_r(S)),\beta(q_r(Y))\) to \(F\). Their mod-\(2\) Frattini classes are respectively those of \(A,S,Y\), so they define an automorphism
\(\varphi\in\operatorname{Aut}(F)\). It satisfies
\[
q_0\varphi=\beta q_r,\qquad \rho\varphi=\rho.
\]
Taking kernels gives
\[
\varphi\!\left(\overline{\langle\!\langle r\rangle\!\rangle}\right)
 =
\overline{\langle\!\langle r_0\rangle\!\rangle},
\]
as required.

Finally, Lemma 3.5 should record not only the marked abelianization and cup form, but also
\[
\chi_2(\bar a,\bar s,\bar y)
 =
\bigl(-1,1,(-3)^{-1}\bigr).
\]
This follows from local reciprocity:
\(\chi_2(\operatorname{rec}(u))=u^{-1}\) for \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), while the uniformizer component acts trivially on the \(2\)-power cyclotomic extension. Thus the actual local relator satisfies the added hypothesis. The manuscript already isolates Proposition 3.4 as its sole missing insertion and derives the required marked local presentation from it.  Consequently the displayed candidate presentation remains unchanged. 

ESTABLISHED — The principal-unit automorphism exists uniformly for every \(u\in\mathbf Z_2^\times\), via the cyclotomic tangential action on the three-puncture group followed by lifting through \(C=A^2\); the orientation-corrected Proposition 3.4 follows.

GAP — The current manuscript still contains the false un-oriented statement and has not yet incorporated the cyclotomic square-root lemma or the orientation row in Lemma 3.5.

NEXT — Replace Proposition 3.4 by the oriented version above and insert the two-lemma proof: peripheral cyclotomic powering, then square-root/HNN lifting.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
